title: Software Developer
description: Build web, mobile, and desktop applications. Software developers write, test, and maintain code across the full stack — from user interfaces to databases and APIs.

employability.demand: Very high — ICT skills shortage; software engineers listed on SA Critical Skills List 2025.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; demand outpaces supply across all sectors. Remote work opens global opportunities.
pathways: BSc Computer Science / BSc IT (3–4 years)

title: Data Scientist / Analyst
description: Extract insights from large datasets using statistics, machine learning, and data visualisation. Inform business decisions and build predictive models across every industry.

employability.demand: Very high — data science is the fastest-growing ICT discipline; on Critical Skills List.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; every data-driven organisation needs analysts and scientists.
pathways: BSc Statistics / Data Science / Computer Science (3–4 years)

title: AI / Machine Learning Engineer
description: Design and deploy machine learning models and AI systems — from natural language processing to computer vision. Bridges data science and production software engineering.

employability.demand: Very high — AI talent is globally scarce; SA companies competing for local expertise.
employability.outlook: Explosive; every industry is integrating AI tools.
pathways: BSc Computer Science / BSc AI (3–4 years)
Tech companies, financial services, research labs

title: Cybersecurity Specialist
description: Protect systems, networks, and data from cyber threats. Roles span penetration testing, incident response, security architecture, and compliance — increasingly critical as attacks escalate.

employability.demand: Very high — cyber threats growing; acute shortage of qualified specialists.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; cybersecurity spend is rising across government, finance, and healthcare.
pathways: BSc Computer Science / BSc IT (3–4 years)

title: Network & Systems Engineer
description: Design, implement, and maintain computer networks and server infrastructure — LANs, WANs, cloud infrastructure, and telecoms networks. Keep organisations connected and running.

employability.demand: High — network infrastructure is the backbone of every organisation.
employability.outlook: Steady; cloud migration and 5G rollout creating new specialist demand.
pathways: BSc IT / Diploma in IT (3 years)
ISPs, corporates, government, managed service providers

title: Business / Systems Analyst
description: Bridge the gap between business needs and IT solutions — gathering requirements, mapping processes, and specifying systems. Work closely with developers, stakeholders, and project managers.

employability.demand: High — every digital transformation project needs business analysts.
employability.outlook: Growing; listed on several critical skills frameworks.
pathways: BSc IT / BCom Information Systems (3–4 years)
Banks, consulting firms, government, tech companies

title: Cloud Solutions Architect
description: Design and oversee cloud infrastructure — choosing services, planning migrations, and ensuring security and cost-efficiency on platforms like AWS, Azure, and GCP.

employability.demand: Very high — cloud adoption accelerating; architects are scarce.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; cloud is the default for most new systems.
pathways: BSc Computer Science / BSc IT (3–4 years)
Consulting firms, banks, tech companies, remote contracts

title: DevOps / Platform Engineer
description: Automate software delivery pipelines, manage infrastructure as code, and maintain CI/CD systems. Keep development and operations teams aligned for fast, reliable software releases.

employability.demand: High — DevOps practices are now standard in software teams.
employability.outlook: Strong; demand grows with software team sizes.
pathways: BSc Computer Science / BSc IT (3–4 years)

title: Database Administrator
description: Design, maintain, and optimise databases that store critical data. DBAs ensure data integrity, performance, backups, and security across relational and NoSQL systems.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — needed in every data-heavy organisation.
employability.outlook: Stable; increasingly complemented by data engineering skills.
pathways: BSc IT / BCom Information Systems (3–4 years)

title: IT Support Technician
description: Provide first- and second-line support to users — diagnosing hardware and software problems, setting up devices, and maintaining IT infrastructure in organisations of all sizes.

employability.demand: High — every organisation with computers needs IT support.
employability.outlook: Stable; a strong entry point into ICT with clear progression paths.
pathways: Diploma in IT / N+ / A+ certification
